response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 15+ years of development experience, they are fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. C++ production experience ended over a decade ago (only 6 months in 2014-2015), and their entire recent career has focused exclusively on web/fullstack development using TypeScript, Node.js, and React. The role requires deep C++ expertise with STL, networking, and multi-threading—none of which are evident in recent work. Additionally, there is no experience with industrial automation, embedded systems, IoT protocols like MQTT, secure development lifecycle practices, or IEC 62443 standards, all of which are fundamental to this position.
strengths: Strong software engineering practices with proven expertise in TDD, CI/CD, code quality, and testing methodologies
Extensive development experience spanning 15+ years with demonstrated ability to learn diverse tech stacks and mentor team members
Solid exposure to high-performance, low-latency systems through microservices architecture work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
concerns: C++ production experience is over 10 years old with only 6 months at National Instruments; recent career focused entirely on web/fullstack development with TypeScript and Node.js, not listed in current skills
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, embedded systems, IoT protocols (MQTT), industrial cybersecurity, or IEC 62443 compliance standards

Career trajectory shows clear divergence from systems programming toward web development; lacks the systems-level networking, multi-threading, and concurrent programming expertise required for on-premises and cloud platform solutions

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and embedded systems background
Software engineering discipline and mentoring capability
